notes: Perfect score (14/14) with skill vs 36% (5/14) without — large and consistent gap across all eval types.

The --force flag on clasp push is the most reliably discriminating assertion: without_skill misses it in all 3 evals that test it (1, 2, 4). It's a nuanced but critical detail that only the skill captures.

Project-specific context (Script ID, file layout, email) is completely absent without the skill (eval 3: 1/5). This is expected — the skill's main value in setup scenarios is carrying this institutional knowledge.
Without_skill partially passes eval 2 (2/3) because push-before-run order and auth awareness are general clasp knowledge, not skill-specific. The skill adds the --force flag detail.
Time cost: ~94s additional per invocation with skill vs without (140s vs 46s) — expected due to skill content loading into context. Acceptable tradeoff for the accuracy improvement.
No eval was fully passed without the skill; all 4 evals were fully passed with the skill. The skill is unambiguously load-bearing.
